Android emulator support is available today for Android developers everywhere on Devin.
Scale up Android workflows with cloud agents - a huge step for Android devs!
Google has added native Android app generation to AI Studio.
Developers can now build complete Kotlin-based Android apps with Jetpack Compose from a single text prompt — no Android Studio install, no SDK download required.
The launch supports personal utility apps, hardware-enabled apps using GPS/Bluetooth/NFC/camera, and Gemini-integrated AI apps.
Also added build timing regression detection."},{"url":"https://cognition.com/blog/android-emulator","title":"Devin now supports Android emulators | Cognition","content":"Devin can now spin up an Android Virtual Device (AVD), enabling autonomous development for Android applications.
This is available now for teams using Devin."},{"url":"https://docs.devin.ai/onboard-devin/environment/android-emulation","title":"Android emulator support","content":"Use an `x86_64` system image for best performance inside Devin's environment.
Devin will launch the emulator (if it isn't already running), build and run your app, and interact with it — using `adb` for programmatic actions and Computer Use for visual interactions.
Android emulator support plugs directly into Devin's Testing & Recordings workflow.
For example, include the Gradle build command, which activity to launch, and which flows to verify.
After testing your Android app, Devin writes down what it learned — how to start the emulator, which Gradle tasks to run, how to navigate to the feature under test — and proposes creating or updating a Skill via PR.
Over time, this means Devin gets better at testing your Android project.
Also check that your blueprint installs the emulator and a compatible `x86_64` system image."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/jefflinwang_android-is-now-available-on-devin-as-agent-activity-7460410898864807936-4Qt5","title":"Android is now available on Devin As agent capabilities become better and better you can hand off more end to end work to Devin. For teams building Android applications, this completes an important part of the development loop."},{"url":"https://medium.com/@anandwana/android-ai-in-2026-the-shift-every-developer-should-understand-2f6f769e283e","title":"Medium","content":"In 2026, that definition feels outdated.
With Gemini Nano, AICore, and ML Kit GenAI, Android apps can now run useful AI features locally on the device.
This may be the most important developer shift.
This does not mean developers are being replaced.
Use Gemini Nano and AICore when you want fast, private, on-device AI.
Use ML Kit GenAI when you want common AI features like summarization, rewriting, proofreading, and smart replies.
Use Gemini in Android Studio and Google AI Studio to speed up your workflow.
This is the Android shift of 2026.
AppFunctions when agents need access."}]}
